text.title: Discovery Cadence Design Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Discovery Cadence Design application must enforce. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Reserve 1 interview slot per PM per week as a recurring calendar block. Without a recurring slot, discovery decays to zero in 4-8 weeks.
rule.rationale: Teresa Torres + Erika Hall data: PMs without recurring slot drop to 0 interviews/month within a quarter.

rule.statement: Opportunity Solution Tree refreshed biweekly (not on-demand). Refresh = add new opportunities + retire solved ones + reprioritize.
rule.rationale: OST without refresh becomes stale wishlists; biweekly is the cadence that keeps it operational.

rule.statement: Maintain a queue of ≥3 active assumption tests. Each has a hypothesis, test method, success criteria, decision after.
rule.rationale: Discovery without assumption testing = vibes; explicit queue creates pipeline of evidence.

rule.statement: Share interview transcripts + OST + assumption tests in a shared space (Confluence/Notion/Linear). Discovery is a team sport; private notes do not compound.
rule.rationale: Tacit knowledge in PM heads is the #1 risk to discovery practice; shared space converts to org capability.

rule.statement: After 90 days, evaluate: interviews/month, assumptions tested, decisions changed by discovery. Anything <0.5 / month is a broken cadence.
rule.rationale: Cadence design is hypothesis; explicit metric prevents ritual-without-effect.
